item.title: Why Republicans Aren’t Condemning Trump’sMeet the PressWalkout
item.description: Denying the legitimacy of vote-counting has become party doctrine.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The White Identitarians Are Having a Moment
item.description: Now that DEI and anti-racism are in retreat, they’re moving on to a more ambitious goal.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Chatterton Williams

item.title: The ‘Battle Hymn’ Can’t Be Ignored
item.description: The rousing urgency of the song seems to possess a power that, according to its own lyrics, “transfigures you and me.”
item.dc:creator: Jake Lundberg

item.title: How America Gave Up on Its Own History
item.description: Unable to agree on how to interpret the American story, the country’s schools, universities, and political institutions have stopped trying to tell it at all.
item.dc:creator: Yoni Appelbaum

item.title: Ukraine Is Not Losing. Russia Is Not Winning.
item.description: A momentum shift that changes everything
item.dc:creator: Anne Applebaum

item.title: Cut Platner Loose
item.description: Just a few years ago, Republicans sacrificed an Alabama Senate seat rather than endure an unacceptable colleague. Will Democrats do the same in Maine?
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: American Democracy Wasn’t Designed for This
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Rosen

item.title: The Supreme Court Has Invented a Right to Discriminate
item.description: Alabama gambled on the Court’s partisanship, and won.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: The Republicans Who Impugn Talarico’s Manhood
item.description: Attacks on the Democratic Senate candidate in Texas show the GOP’s narrow, anxious definition of masculinity.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: Yet More Damning Revelations About Graham Platner
item.description: Voters may be questioning the Senate candidate’s claims that he is a changed man.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig
